Battery charging over-voltage protection point (converted to the voltage corresponding to 12 V, followed by the same battery voltage)
Lead-acid battery is prohibited from boost charge, and lithium battery is prohibited from over-charging voltage
After the battery enters floating charging, the battery voltage is lower than the judged point again, and the battery enters boost charge again
After the battery is protected from over-discharge and under-voltage, it is returned to the discharged state

Alarming of low battery voltage with load cut-off
During the battery over-discharge delay, the battery voltage is lower than the judged point, and then the load is off at once
The load is switched to the AC power when the battery voltage falls below this judged point
Only the lithium battery is effective, and when the current of constant-voltage charging state is lower than this value, the charging is stopped
When the SOC capacity is greater than or equal to this value, charging is stopped; valid for BMS communication
With the alarming of low SOC capacity; valid for BMS communication
In SBU mode, AC power is applied when SOC is less than or equal to this value
In SBU mode, inverter is applied when SOC is greater than or equal to this value
When battery voltage is higher than this judged point, inverter switches back

During discharging period, discharging stops when SOC < specified value

The power data for each day occupies one Address, so for example, if today is September 27, the PV power generation data for the last 7 days is as follows: F000: power generation on September 26 (yesterday), F001: power generation on September 25 (two days ago), F002: power generation on September 24, ..., F006: power generation on September 20
